text: The quiet, clinical corridor gives me a sense of detachment—a liminal space, empty and echoing, both literally and existentially. Then, the shift to the vivid wall marked R+D makes me contemplative. Am I witnessing the boundary between observation and innovation? It feels as though I am on the threshold of meaning, where sterile silence yields to the colorful pursuit of knowledge. There is anticipation here, beneath the surface boredom, as if the world is about to whisper a secret if only I linger long enough to listen.
